title: Concise printable/offline 'tenant rights' wallet card (<= grade 6) — cited, attorney-reviewed
context: People often need to know their rights in a moment with no connectivity and under stress. Know-Your-Rights produces a concise, printable wallet card — one page, offline-usable, at or below grade 6 reading level — covering the highest-value tenant rights and the most important 'what to do / who to call' steps. Like all content, it is general legal information (a plain-language summary of what the law of the named jurisdiction says), not individualized advice. Claims trace to the explainer set (task 009) sources. Requires attorney sign-off before delivery. Depends on task 009.
objective: Draft and iterate to final form a concise, one-page, printable, offline-usable tenant-rights wallet card for the pilot jurisdiction at or below grade 6 reading level. The card covers the highest-value rights (from the six explainer topics) and the key 'what to do / who to call' steps. All claims trace to the vetted sources from task 006. The card carries the 'not legal advice' disclaimer, a 'verify currency' note, and a lastVerified stamp. Requires licensed-attorney sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: One-page wallet-card format: legible when printed on a single page or card, usable offline with no connectivity required
Reading level at or below grade 6 (Flesch-Kincaid or equivalent); verified by the readability check from the content-lint suite (task 004)

Covers the highest-value tenant rights across the six core topics (eviction notice, habitability, deposits, lockouts, retaliation, discrimination) distilled to actionable plain-language statements of general legal information
Includes a 'what to do / who to call' section with at least two concrete referral options (local legal aid, housing court self-help, or relevant tenant agency)
All claims on the card trace back to the vetted primary sources from task 006 (via the explainer set, task 009); no uncited claim appears
Carries the 'not legal advice' disclaimer, a 'verify currency — laws change' note, and a lastVerified date stamp
Passes the content-lint suite (task 004): non-advice lint, neutrality lint, jurisdiction-label check, citation-coverage check, and readability check
Licensed-attorney sign-off recorded (attorney admitted in the pilot jurisdiction) before delivery; sign-off version-scoped to this content version
output: A one-page printable, offline-usable tenant-rights wallet card for the pilot jurisdiction — reading level at or below grade 6, primary-source-cited, lint-clean, carrying the 'not legal advice' disclaimer and lastVerified stamp, and licensed-attorney-signed-off.
